rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE AMENDED AND RESTATED ROBERT M. PICCININI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court are two verified applications for admission pro hac vice: Verified Application of Brian K. Lee for Admission Pro Hac Vice; and Verified Application of Eric P. Magnuson for Admission Pro Hac Vice. No objection to Brian K. Lee, Esq.'s application was filed. No one served any response to Brian K. Lee, Esq.'s application prior to the hearing. No objection to Eric P. Magnuson, Esq.'s application was filed. No one served any response to Eric P. Magnuson, Esq.'s application prior to the hearing. The unopposed Verified Application of Brian K. Lee for Admission Pro Hac Vice is GRANTED, despite statutory procedural defects. The unopposed Verified Application of Eric P. Magnuson for Admission Pro Hac Vice is GRANTED, despite statutory procedural defects. Under CRC Rule 9.40(c), A person desiring to appear as counsel pro hac vice in a superior court must file with the court a verified application together with proof of service by mail in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure section 1013a of a copy of the application and of the notice of hearing of the application on all parties who have appeared in the cause and on the State Bar of California at its San Francisco office. The notice of hearing must be given at the time prescribed in Code of Civil Procedure section 1005 unless the court has prescribed a shorter period. A copy of each application and notice of hearing were not served by mail on all parties who have appeared in this matter, nor on the State Bar of California at their San Francisco office. Neither Verified Application is in compliance with CRC Rule 9.40(c)(1), CRC Rule 9.40(e), CCP section 1013.1(a), or CCP section 1005. The Verified Application of Brian K. Lee for Admission Pro Hac Vice and Verified Application of Eric P. Magnuson for Admission Pro Hac Vice are procedurally defective. Substantively, however, both Verified Application of Brian K. Lee for Admission Pro Hac Vice and Verified Application of Eric P. Magnuson for Admission Pro Hac Vice meet the statutory requirements, as follows. California requires that an attorney applying for admission pro hac vice (1) be in good standing and eligible to practice before the bar of any U.S. court or the highest court of any state, territory, or U.S. insular possession; (2) be retained to appear in the specific case pending in California; and (3) be associated with an active member of the State Bar of California who serves as attorney of record in the specific case pending in California. The attorney is ineligible if they reside in California, are regularly employed in California, or regularly engage in substantial business, professional, or other activities in California. CRC Rule 9.40 lists the requirements for the application for admission pro hac vice. If the moving party has met the requirements and included all requisite information, the court should grant the application for admission pro hac vice. The papers show that both applicants have met all requirements stated under CRC Rule 9.40(a) and that neither is ineligible based on residence, employment, or substantial activity in California. Both applicants included all requisite information in their respective application for admission pro hac vice. Both applications are unopposed, verified, and substantively compliant with the applicable law. Rather than denying the applications and requiring refiling with statutorily-compliant service, the Court exercises it discretion to waive the procedural defects in service and grant both applications, in the interests of judicial economy and efficiency. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. before the hearing.

rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
